title: With spears & swords, Nihangs stay locked inside Uttarakhand gurdwara
content: A tense standoff at a Uttarakhand gurdwara entered its third day with Nihang Sikhs holding out, reportedly demanding the release of four arrested individuals. Punjab's Chief Minister has urged an amicable resolution through talks. While police maintain the incident is unrelated to a prior clash in Karnaprayag, a Sikh organization suggests the dispute stemmed from accommodation issues for pilgrims heading to Hemkund Sahib.

title: Illegal Lucknow building had 2016 demolition order, someone revoked it within 60 days
content: A devastating fire has cast a shadow over a Lucknow building's past. Documents reveal a 2016 demolition order for unauthorized construction was controversially revoked within two months. The property, initially for residential use, was later allegedly used commercially, a key focus in the ongoing fire investigation. Officials are probing the circumstances surrounding the order's reversal.

title: 2 charred to death, 2 trapped as massive fire erupts at Andhra energy plant
content: Tragedy struck Dakshin Energy's Paravada facility Tuesday morning as a massive fire claimed the lives of two young workers, Trinadh (27) and Venkatesh (24). Two others are feared trapped amidst the inferno, which is believed to have been fueled by the large plastic inventory. Firefighting efforts are ongoing, with investigations into the cause set to commence once the blaze is contained.

title: 'Not spending time thinking about my age': Messi after rewriting World Cup history
content: Lionel Messi shattered FIFA World Cup records, becoming the all-time leading goalscorer and most victorious player just before his 39th birthday. Despite the milestones, Messi emphasised his focus remains on fitness and feeling good, not his age. He expressed uncertainty about playing in Argentina's next match but praised the team's competitive spirit, even after a missed penalty initially disrupted their rhythm.

title: ‘Won't return to pre-war conditions’: Iran says Hormuz will be administered by them
content: In a bold statement, Iran's chief negotiator, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, affirmed Tehran's control over the crucial Strait of Hormuz, dismissing any pre-war stipulations. Concurrently, negotiations in Switzerland culminated in securing $12 billion worth of Iranian assets stuck overseas. Additionally, Iran consented to create a communication framework to monitor maritime activities in the vital waterway, aimed at preventing possible clashes and misunderstandings.

title: Germany sends a clear message to America: NASA can’t operate without us
content: Germany highlights Europe's crucial role in US space endeavors, stating missions like lunar flights are impossible without European technology. This assertion comes amid broader EU efforts to achieve "tech sovereignty" and reduce reliance on American giants in cloud, AI, and microchips. Concerns over US tech access, amplified by recent AI model bans, are driving Europe to develop its own alternatives and bolster domestic capacity, aiming to reclaim global geoeconomic influence.

title: 'I've zero goals in two World Cups': Zlatan bows to Messi's record-breaking march
content: After Messi scored twice in Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria on Monday to become the highest goalscorer in FIFA World Cup history, Ibrahimovic reflected on the 38-year-old's achievement.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: From IPO boom to bond debut: Elon Musk's SpaceX seeks funds for AI and starship
content: SpaceX is tapping the bond market for the first time, seeking funds for its ambitious AI and next-gen space tech projects, even as it boasts over $100 billion in cash. This move allows capital raising without diluting shareholder control. The announcement comes amid a significant drop in SpaceX shares post-IPO, which nonetheless propelled Elon Musk to trillionaire status. The company's expansion into AI and Starship development is driving substantial investment.

title: FIFA World Cup: Erling Haaland's double sends Norway into knockouts
content: Erling Haaland's sensational brace secured Norway's passage to the World Cup Round of 32 in a dramatic 3-2 win over Senegal.  The prolific striker now boasts four goals in the tournament, keeping pace in the Golden Boot race.  Norway's victory, their first World Cup qualification since 1998, sets up a crucial group decider against France.

title: After laying off 100 of engineers, GM replaces 1,000 workers with 50 robots at flagship US Plant
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock market recommendations for June 23, 2026
content: Stock market recommendations: Delhivery, Lodha Developers, REC Ltd, and Indian Energy Exchange - these are the top stocks to buy recommended by Somil Mehta, Head of Retail Research at Mirae Asset Sharekhan for June 23, 2026:

title: Messi scores from 20 yards, sets record for most goals in FIFA WC history - Watch
content: Messi reached the landmark in the 38th minute, just two days before his 39th birthday. With the strike, Messi scored in his sixth straight World Cup match, joining France's Just Fontaine and Brazil's Jairzinho as the only players to achieve that feat.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
content: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: New Champions crowned, shocking betrayal headlines explosive WWE Raw Night
content: London's O2 Arena witnessed a dramatic WWE RAW go-home show for Night of Champions.  A beloved star turned heel, shocking fans, while Chad Gable faced his former stablemates.  Key bouts saw LA Knight triumph over Jimmy Uso amidst Bloodline interference, and The Street Profits capture the WWE World Tag Team Titles in a chaotic main event, culminating in a surprise championship coronation.

title: Who is Geno Smith’s alleged Ex? Inside the claims stirring NFL attention
content: The New York Jets’ star player, Geno Smith, was gearing up for the next season when he found himself in major legal trouble after an alleged ex girlfriend accused him of domestic violence and being neglectful of a child just a few hours ago. The controversial accusations have sparked shockwaves across the league and many are curious about Geno Smith’s alleged ex girlfriend.

title: France win 3-0 as Mbappé scores brace on 100th cap; Dembele nets first WC goal
content: France defeated Iraq 3-0 in Philadelphia to secure their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout rounds, with Kylian Mbappe scoring twice on his 100th international appearance. The match was halted for 130 minutes due to severe thunderstorms and lightning at half-time. Ousmane Dembele also scored as France maintained a perfect group-stage record.

title: Don’t stop your kids from joining protest, says CJP
content: The Cockroach Janta Party's protest demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG paper leak entered its third day. The group held a candlelight vigil for students who allegedly died by suicide, urging the government to provide ₹1 crore compensation to their families. They also appealed to parents to allow their children to join the agitation, emphasizing the protest targets governance failure, not individuals.

title: Will CSK have a new head coach next season? Big update emerges
content: Chennai Super Kings are set to retain head coach Stephen Fleming despite recent fan backlash following two disappointing IPL seasons. Fleming, a long-time figure with the franchise, is expected to have his contract extended. This decision underscores the strong influence of MS Dhoni and their enduring professional bond.  Meanwhile, CSK has dismissed rumors of player trades, including any involving Hardik Pandya.

title: ‘Messi is a joke!’ : Rooney’s 14-year-old tweet resurfaces after Argentine's heroics
content: As Lionel Messi continues to bask in his 2026 World Cup glory, Wayne Rooney's 2012 tweet dubbing Messi a 'joke' yet the 'best ever' has resurfaced, stirring conversations among fans. Messi's remarkable two-goal game against Austria catapulted him to the position of all-time leading men’s World Cup scorer with 18 goals, breaking Klose's long-standing record, and prompting fans to celebrate Rooney's earlier prediction.

title: Scent of a scarf: Sniffer dog helps crack rape case of 6-year-old in UP's Sambhal
content: A remarkable seven-year-old Labrador, Mary, proved instrumental in apprehending a suspect in a disturbing sexual assault case in Sambhal. With no CCTV or eyewitnesses, investigators relied on Mary's keen sense of smell. She expertly tracked a scent from a recovered scarf, leading police directly to the accused's residence. The swift operation, culminating in the suspect's capture and confession, highlights the invaluable role of canine units in solving complex crimes.

title: Messi scripts FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player ever to...
content: Lionel Messi became the outright highest scorer in FIFA World Cup history after netting his 17th goal against Austria, surpassing Miroslav Klose. The Argentina captain also equalled the record of scoring in six consecutive World Cup matches. France star Kylian Mbappe praised Messi, calling him and Cristiano Ronaldo the best, while insisting his own focus remains on winning another World Cup.

title: 'Maa Inti Bangaaram' BO day 4: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's film drops on Monday
content: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's 'Maa Inti Bangaaram' has garnered a respectable Rs 27.20 crore net in India after four days. While the film saw a dip on Monday, its opening weekend collections were strong, reaching Rs 10.10 crore on Sunday. Overseas markets have pushed the worldwide gross to over Rs 46 crore, with the Telugu version driving most of the domestic business.

title: NEET row: Student asked to remove sacred thread, Assam orders probe against two cops
content: Assam Police have launched an inquiry into a DSP and a havildar after a Brahmin student was allegedly forced to remove his sacred thread during a NEET re-test frisking in Nalbari. A magisterial probe confirmed the student identified the personnel involved. The district administration stated there was no justification for the action, and the student was allowed to take the exam after handing the thread to his mother.

content: The Pentagon has told senators it needs roughly $80 billion, mostly to cover the cost of the US war against Iran, adding to what is already a sizable military spending boost being sought by President Donald Trump.

title: Soldier ends life at Nagrota Army camp
content:
Representative image

JAMMU: A 29-year-old soldier died allegedly by suicide at an Army camp in Nagrota, on the outskirts of Jammu, early Monday, officials said. Police said Abhijit Nandan was found hanging at his place of posting. Authorities took him to the military hospital in Satwari, where doctors declared him dead. The body was later shifted to Jammu GMCH for medico-legal formalities. A team of cops visited the scene and collected evidence. Further investigation was underway to establish the reason behind the deceased’s drastic step, officials said.

End of Article

title: Four fertiliser ships exit Strait of Hormuz, head to India
content:
Four fertiliser ships exit Hormuz

NEW DELHI: Four ships carrying around 1.8 lakh tonnes of urea, di-ammonium phosphate (DAP) and sulphur have crossed the Strait of Hormuz and are now heading to ports in India, the fertiliser department said on Monday. The ships crossed the conflict-hit sea channel last week. TOI has learnt they sailed through the strait on Thursday, Friday and Saturday. Three oil tankers also crossed the critical sea route Saturday. Officials said the seven India-bound ships crossed the strait just before Iran announced its fresh closure. While two ships loaded with fertilisers are carrying 91,750 tonnes of urea, another vessel is transporting 55,000 tonnes of DAP and the fourth one has 33,251 tonnes of sulphur. They are likely to reach ports in Krishnapatnam, Kakinada (Andhra), Paradeep (Odisha) and Mundra (Gujarat) soon.

End of Article

title: 'Deeply saddened': Jaishankar condoles death of 12 Indians in Qatar blast
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: 'First shoot us, then build dam': Tribals oppose revival of decade-old pending project in Bastar
content: Rashmi is a Special Correspondent with The Times of India in Chhattisgarh. She covers Politics, Left Wing Extremism, Crime and Human Rights among other areas of news value.

title: 12 Indians among 13 killed in Qatar gas plant blast; accident says minister
content: 12 Indians were among 13 people killed in an explosion at a gas processing plant in the Ras Laffan Industrial City in Qatar . 66 people who have been reported injured are receiving medical treatment, none of whom are in life-threatening condition . authorities are investigating the cause of the incident .
